title: celtis occidentalis subsp. tenuifolia
intro: Celtis occidentalis subsp. tenuifolia, also known as the narrow-leafed hackberry, is a subspecies of the deciduous hackberry tree native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Celtis occidentalis subsp. tenuifolia include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting immune function, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although proper identification and preparation are essential to ensure safety and efficacy.
